sentence_reviews.text: The learner chooses informative data or follows an ordered progression from easier to harder experience.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.6.5 'Active learning' and Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples'.
sentence_reviews.notes: Active learning is directly named; curriculum learning and easier-to-harder progression are not directly supported by the inspected AIMA evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Active learning & curriculum learning is to connect Supervised learning, Datasets, sampling & splits to downstream capabilities including Curricula, data mixtures & sampling schedules, AutoML & automated model design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples', 19.6.5 'Active learning', and learning-system/data-management entries; PML book1 long TOC data, validation, and learning topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supervised/data aspects and active learning are supported, but curriculum learning, AutoML, and the full downstream graph role are not directly established.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Curricula, data mixtures & sampling schedules
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, active learning under learning with less data; PML book1 long TOC data, validation, and sampling/statistics topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sampling/data selection is supported, but curricula and data mixtures are not directly named in inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for AutoML & automated model design
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, machine-learning system development/model selection entries and active learning topic.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence does not directly support active/curriculum learning as a substrate for AutoML or automated model design.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Supervised learning, Datasets, sampling & splits.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, supervised learning and active learning; PML book1 data/validation topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependencies are plausible, but the deployment-objective mismatch limitation is generic atlas wording not directly supported by the cited locations.

sentence_reviews.text: Active learning & curriculum learning follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples', 19.2 'Supervised Learning', and 19.6.5 'Active learning'.
sentence_reviews.notes: Active learning appears under learning from examples; curriculum learning is not directly named, so the combined-node rationale is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Active learning & curriculum learning uses Datasets, sampling & splits as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA active learning under learning with less data; PML data and validation/sampling topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Choosing informative examples and ordering data imply dataset/sampling dependence; train/validation/test splits are broader than the direct evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Active learning & curriculum learning uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, local search/optimization and learning chapters; PML optimization/model-selection topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Optimization is relevant to learner training and selection policies, but the inspected evidence does not directly establish it as a dependency for active/curriculum learning.

edge_reviews.notes: Active learning is commonly framed in supervised example-label acquisition. trained_under/common is defensible for the combined node.
Data selection/order is central to active and curriculum learning; uses/core is defensible.
Optimization is relevant but not directly shown as a prerequisite by inspected evidence. uses/common is plausible.
